channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: 14 members of an Australian religious group are convicted over diabetic girl's death
item.description: BRISBANE, Australia (AP) — Two bereaved parents and 12 fellow members of an Australian religious congregation accused of killing an 8-year-old girl by withholding her diabetes medication were found guilty on Wednesday of manslaughter. Elizabeth Rose Struhs died on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Year of the Snake is underway with Lunar New Year festivities in Asia and around the world
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Lunar New Year festivals and prayers marked the start of the Year of the Snake around Asia and farther afield on Wednesday — including in Moscow.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil to set up deportee reception center after contentious flight from US
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — The Brazilian government Tuesday said it will create a reception center for deported migrants from the United States following controversy over conditions on a recent deportation flight.
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES and DAVID BILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Several people are feared dead in a stampede at the massive Maha Kumbh festival in India
item.description: PRAYAGRAJ, India (AP) — Several people were feared dead and many more injured in a stampede early Wednesday as tens of thousands of Hindus rushed to take ritual baths in sacred rivers at the massive Maha Kumbh festival in northern India.
item.dc:creator: RAJESH KUMAR SINGH and SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: An unlikely symbol of cooperation, the barn owl brings Arab and Israeli scientists together
item.description: A cross-border conservation project centered on barn owls is fostering cooperation between Israeli and Arab scientists, offering a rare example of coexistence in the conflict-ridden Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Derek Gatopoulos, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Maha Kumbh festival in northern India is the world's largest religious gathering
item.description: PRAYAGRAJ, India (AP) — Hindu devotees, mystics and holy men and women have gathered in India's northern city of Prayagraj for the Maha Kumbh festival, the world's largest religious gathering. The festival is held every 12 years at the confluence of sacred rivers.
item.dc:creator: SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Denmark to increase military spending in North Atlantic by $2 billion amid row with Trump over Greenland
item.description: Denmark is increasing military spending in the North Atlantic amid President Donald Trump’s bid to have Greenland sold or ceded to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korean leader Kim inspects nuclear facility as Pyongyang pressures Trump administration
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un has inspected a facility that produces nuclear material and called for bolstering the country’s nuclear capability, state media reports.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Colombian customs official pleads guilty in money laundering case tied to DEA misconduct
item.description: TAMPA, Florida (AP) — A Colombian customs worker admitted his role Tuesday in taking bribes and funneling more than $1 million in drug proceeds in a case that threatened to expose dirty dealings between U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration agents and their informants.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN and JIM MUSTIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The discovery of brutal mass graves in Syria reveals Assad's legacy of horror
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — The charred remains of at least 26 victims of the Bashar Assad government were located Tuesday by Syrian civil defense workers in two separate basements in rural Damascus.
item.dc:creator: OMAR ALBAM AND SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US ships return to Red Sea amid Gaza ceasefire
item.description: U.S. ships are returning to the Red Sea following promises from Yemen’s Houthi rebels to abstain from attacks on American and British vessels. The pledge, which comes after more than a year of shipping traffic disruption on a major international trade route, was made on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Israeli President Herzog highlights antisemitism in UN speech as new report shows shocking trend
item.description: Israel's President Isaac Herzog addressed the U.N. on Monday at its annual Holocaust Remembrance Day event. His speech comes as a new report shows a worrying rise in antisemitic attitudes globally.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US consulate warns of gun battles, IEDs, kidnappings in Mexican border towns near Texas
item.description: U.S. officials have put several Mexican border towns near Texas under the highest-level travel advisory amid gun battles, kidnappings and IEDs in the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A weakened Iran has Iraq looking to curb Tehran-backed extremists in country
item.description: The Iraqi government is looking to gain control of Iranian-aligned armed groups that operate inside of Iraq but outside state control, in yet another sign of Iran's weakened position since October 7.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italy sends 49 more migrants to Albania for processing following earlier court rejections
item.description: An Italian navy ship has arrived in Albania with 49 migrants intercepted in international waters for processing of their asylum applications at special Albanian centers.
item.dc:creator: Erion Xhabafti And Llazar Semini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump previews brute foreign policy approach with Colombia spat
item.description: President Trump is flexing his muscle just a week into his presidency, using tariffs and sanctions as a leverage tool to enact his agenda, even when it involves U.S. allies.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: At Auschwitz memorial, survivors see echoes of the past in rising antisemitism
item.description: OSWIECIM, Poland (AP) — Auschwitz survivors warned Monday of the rising antisemitism and hatred they are witnessing in the modern world as they gathered with world leaders and European royalty on the 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Northern Gaza is shattered. The spirit of returning Palestinians is not
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — They walked for hours loaded with whatever clothes, food and blankets they could carry. Many smiled, some hugged loved ones they hadn’t seen for months. Hundreds of thousands of Palestinians filled Gaza’s main coastal road as they streamed back to homes in the north.
item.dc:creator: OSAMA SALEH, ABDEL-KAREEM HANA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EU military chief says it would make sense to put European troops in Greenland, Welt reports
item.description: Top-ranking European military official Robert Brieger reportedly said it would "make perfect sense" to station troops in Greenland amid President Trump's interest in acquiring it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump speaks with India's Modi about trade, future White House visit
item.description: President Trump spoke Monday with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi, discussing the relationship between their two countries and plans for Modi to visit the White House.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: After new CIA finding, China says 'extremely unlikely' COVID-19 came from a lab
item.description: China on Monday dismissed the possibility that the virus that caused COVID-19 leaked from a lab, after the CIA said it now favors the so-called lab leak theory over natural transmission.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, The Hill

item.title: Belarus' President Lukashenko extends rule after election rejected by opposition, EU
item.description: Belarus held an orchestrated election over the weekend that the opposition and the EU rejected as a farce, extending President Lukashenko's more than 30 years in power.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinians stream back to northern Gaza after Israel lifts closure
item.description: Thousands of Palestinians are returning to the northern Gaza Strip Monday as part of the cease-fire and hostage release deal between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Holocaust survivor recalls mother's encounter with Nazi
item.description: Marianne Muller joins “NewsNation Prime” to share her family's story of courage and survival during the Holocaust.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: Romanian prehistoric gold treasures stolen from Dutch museum
item.description: The Geto-Dacian artifacts belonged to the National History Museum in the capital Bucharest and were on loan to the Drents Museum in the Netherlands.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: 13 UN peacekeepers, allied soldiers dead in Congo as M23 rebels make gains in key city
item.description: The Rwanda-backed rebel group M23 is battling Congolese government forces in Congo's key city of Goma, causing the international airport there to be evacuated.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukrainian human rights lawyer, Nobel Prize winner urges Trump to show strength and end the war
item.description: Ukrainian human rights attorney and Nobel Peace Prize winner Oleksandra Matviichuk urges President Trump to show strength as he tries to bring the Russia-Ukraine war to an end.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants Egypt and Jordan to take in Palestinians from Gaza. Here's why they are likely to refuse
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump's suggestion that Egypt and Jordan take in Palestinians from the war-ravaged Gaza Strip is likely to be met with a hard “no" from the two U.S. allies and the Palestinians themselves, who fear Israel would never allow them to return.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tabloids apologize to Prince Harry, admit intruding on Princess Diana
item.description: Prince Harry claimed a monumental victory Wednesday as Rupert Murdoch’s U.K. tabloids made an unprecedented apology.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation
